name: Frozen low-level locomotion controller (WheeledLab-derived)
role: The 'implement on top, not in training' layer. A single-agent command-tracking policy that turns the high-level coordination setpoints (waypoint/velocity/heading) into actual steering+throttle for a rigid-body car in Isaac. Trained ONCE, then FROZEN — never part of the multi-agent training loop.
decision: Train this once (single-agent, in Isaac, using WheeledLab's car asset + domain-randomization recipe) to track velocity/heading setpoints, then freeze it. At demo time, each car = frozen high-level policy -> this frozen controller -> rigid-body car. Because it is frozen and outside the coordination training, there is NO body-transfer problem — we learn coordination on top of a fixed locomotion skill.
license: your code; built on wheeledlab (BSD-3) + Isaac Lab (BSD-3)
inputs: high-level setpoints (target waypoint / velocity / heading) from the trained coordination policy
outputs: steering + throttle commands to the rigid-body car
reality_checks: WheeledLab does NOT ship a generic setpoint tracker — its policies are task-specific (drift/elevation/visual-nav) and downloadable weights are unconfirmed.
So plan to TRAIN this controller yourself (single-agent, fast) using WheeledLab's car + recipe, not download it.
It runs in Isaac/PhysX (WheeledLab-native) — do not expect Isaac-trained weights to run unchanged elsewhere.
risks: if WheeledLab weights/asset aren't usable, this becomes a from-scratch locomotion training (still single-agent, modest)
tracking error feeds the kinematic<->physics gap -> tune limits to match kinematic-env
